name: Federal National Mortgage Association
description: The Federal National Mortgage Association, commonly known as Fannie Mae, is a United States government-sponsored enterprise (GSE) and a leading source of financing for mortgage lenders. Established in 1938 during the Great Depression as part of the New Deal, the corporation's mission is to facilitate equitable and sustainable access to homeownership and quality affordable rental housing. Fannie Mae operates in the secondary mortgage market, purchasing and guaranteeing mortgages from lenders to ensure a reliable and affordable supply of mortgage credit across the nation.
